symptoms.symptom_name: Severe blunt chest trauma with progressive respiratory distress

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mechanism and progressive respiratory distress together indicate a major internal chest injury.
Classic features of significant blood loss.

symptoms.symptom_name: No neck vein distension or tracheal deviation reported

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: These findings would be expected in tension pneumothorax but are absent here, favouring haemothorax; formal examination is needed for certainty.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absent breath sounds, dull percussion, flat neck veins

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective confirmation; clinician-only finding sealed from telehealth chat.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Blunt chest trauma with progressive respiratory distress, pallor, and near-syncope together confirm a major chest injury with shock requiring emergency transport.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate Intensive Care Paramedic dispatch with high-flow oxygen and IV fluid resuscitation en route
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Time-critical management of combined respiratory and haemorrhagic shock.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency intercostal catheter (chest tube) insertion and trauma surgical review on hospital arrival
Waiting to see if breathing improves before calling for help
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Would risk fatal deterioration given progressive respiratory and haemorrhagic shock.
